name: micro
description: A modern and intuitive terminal-based text editor

name: remove
options.description: Remove plugin(s)

name: update
options.description: Update plugin(s) (if no argument is given, updates all plugins)

name: search
options.description: Search for a plugin

name: list
options.description: List installed plugins

name: available
options.description: List available plugins

name: --clean
options.description: Cleans the configuration directory

name: --config-dir
options.description: Specify a custom location for the configuration directory

name: --options
options.description: Show all option help

name: --debug
options.description: Enable debug mode

name: --version
options.description: Show the version number and information

name: --autoindent
options.description: When creating a new line, use the same indentation as the previous line

name: --autosave
options.description: Automatically save the buffer every <n> seconds. If set to 0, no autosaving is performed

name: --autosu
options.description: Automatically attempt to use super user privileges to save without asking

name: --backup
options.description: Automatically keep backups of all open buffers

name: --backupdir
options.description: The directory to place backups in

name: --basename
options.description: In the infobar and tabbar, show only the basename of the file being edited rather than the full path

name: --clipboard
options.description: Apecifies how the system clipboard should be accessed

suggestions.name: external
suggestions.description: Accesses clipboard via an external tool, such as xclip/xsel or wl-clipboard on Linux, pbcopy/pbpaste on MacOS, and system calls on Windows

suggestions.name: terminal
suggestions.description: Accesses the clipboard via your terminal emulator

name: --colorcolumn
options.description: Display a color at the specified column if not set to 0

name: --colorscheme
options.description: Loads the colorscheme stored in $(configDir)/colorschemes/<scheme>.micro

name: --cursorline
options.description: Highlight the line that the cursor is on in a different color

name: --diffgutter
options.description: Display diff indicators before lines

name: --divchars
options.description: Specifies the divider characters used for the dividing line between vertical/horizontal splits (the first character is for vertical dividers, and the second is for horizontal dividers)

name: --divreverse
options.description: Reverse colors specified by the colorscheme

name: --encoding
options.description: The encoding to open and save files with

name: --eofnewline
options.description: Automatically add a newline to the end of the file if one does not exist

name: --fastdirty
options.description: Use 'fast dirty' algorithm to determine if a buffer is modified or not

name: --fileformat
options.description: Type of line endings to be used for the file

name: --filetype
options.description: File type for the current buffer

suggestions.name: unknown
suggestions.description: Automatically overridden depending on type of file opened

name: --ignorecase
options.description: Perform case-insensitive searches

name: --incsearch
options.description: Enable incremental search in "Find" prompt

name: --indentchar
options.description: Sets the indentation character

name: --infobar
options.description: Enables the line at the bottom of the editor where messages are printed

name: --keepautoindent
options.description: Remove trailing whitespace from a line with no insertions when moving to the next line

name: --keymenu
options.description: Display the nano-style key menu at the bottom of the screen

name: --matchbrace
options.description: Underline matching braces for '()', '{}', '[]' when the cursor is on a brace character

name: --mkparents
options.description: Allow parent directories to be created automatically when opening a file on a path that doesn't exist

name: --mouse
options.description: Enable mouse support

name: --paste
options.description: Treat characters sent from the terminal in a single chunk as a paste event rather than a series of manual key presses

name: --parsecursor
options.description: Parse filenames such as file.txt:10:5 as requesting to open file.txt with the cursor at line 10 and column 5

name: --permbackup
options.description: Cause backups to be permanently saved

name: --pluginchannels
options.description: List of URLs pointing to plugin channels for downloading and installing plugins

name: --pluginrepos
options.description: A list of links to plugin repositories

name: --readonly
options.description: Disallow edits to the buffer

name: --relativeruler
options.description: Make line numbers display relatively

name: --rmtrailingws
options.description: Automatically trim trailing whitespaces at ends of lines

name: --ruler
options.description: Display line numbers

name: --savecursor
options.description: Remember where the cursor was last time the file was opened and put it there when re-opened

name: --savehistory
options.description: Remember command history between closing and re-opening micro

name: --saveundo
options.description: Remember undo state between closing and re-opening a file

name: --scrollbar
options.description: Display a scroll bar

name: --scrollmargin
options.description: Margin at which the view starts scrolling when the cursor approaches the edge of the view

name: --scrollspeed
options.description: Number of lines to scroll for one scroll event

name: --smartpaste
options.description: Add leading whitespace when pasting multiple lines

name: --softwrap
options.description: Wrap lines that are too long to fit on the screen

name: --splitbottom
options.description: When a horizontal split is created, create it below the current split

name: --splitright
options.description: When a vertical split is created, create it to the right of the current split

name: --statusformatl
options.description: Format string definition for the left-justified part of the statusline

name: --statusformatr
options.description: Format string definition for the right-justified part of the statusline

name: --statusline
options.description: Display the status line at the bottom of the screen

name: --sucmd
options.description: The super user command

name: --syntax
options.description: Enables syntax highlighting

name: --tabmovement
options.description: Navigate spaces at the beginning of lines as if they are tabs

name: --tabsize
options.description: The size in spaces that a tab character should be displayed with

name: --tabstospaces
options.description: Use spaces instead of tabs

name: --useprimary
options.description: Use the primary clipboard to copy selections in the background

name: --wordwrap
options.description: Wrap long lines by words

name: --xterm
options.description: Assume that the terminal conforms to xterm-256color regardless of what the $TERM variable actually contains
